tools.title: Andrew Yang thinks the next big startup opportunity is lowering the cost of living

tools.title: Anthropic’s safety warnings may have just backfired — the government has pulled the plug on its most powerful AI
tools.summary: Anthropic isn't hiding its frustration. "We disagree that the finding of a narrow potential jailbreak should be cause for recalling a commercial model deployed to hundreds of millions of people," the company wrote in a blog post.
tools.content: Anthropic isn't hiding its frustration. "We disagree that the finding of a narrow potential jailbreak should be cause for recalling a commercial model deployed to hundreds of millions of people," the company wrote in a blog post.

creative.title: New AI model called "Count Anything" does exactly what it says, and that's harder than it sounds
creative.summary: "Count Anything" is intended to be the first AI model capable of counting objects in any type of image, from crowds to cell samples under a microscope, using nothing more than a text prompt. In a comparative test, it cuts the error rate in half compa
creative.content:
"Count Anything" is intended to be the first AI model capable of counting objects in any type of image, from crowds to cell samples under a microscope, using nothing more than a text prompt. In a comparative test, it cuts the error rate in half compared to previous systems. However, the approach still struggles with extremely dense objects and ambiguous terms.
The article New AI model called "Count Anything" does exactly what it says, and that's harder than it sounds appeared first on The Decode

creative.title: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella admits he's a token-maxer, too: "It's addictive"
creative.summary: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella is warning against "token-maxing," throwing the most powerful AI models at every problem. Frontier models shouldn't be wasted on everyday tasks, he says. The marginal cost of productivity gains has to match the token cost.
creative.content:
Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella is warning against "token-maxing," throwing the most powerful AI models at every problem. Frontier models shouldn't be wasted on everyday tasks, he says. The marginal cost of productivity gains has to match the token cost. Still, Nadella admits, "I'm like a token-maxer too. So it is addictive."
The article Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella admits he's a token-maxer, too: "It's addictive" appeared first on The Decoder.

creative.title: Google Research's Gemini-SQL2 tops text-to-SQL benchmarks by a wide margin
creative.summary: Google Research's Gemini-SQL2 turns natural language into executable SQL queries. Built on Gemini 3.1 Pro, it tops the BIRD benchmark at 80.04 percent accuracy, well ahead of OpenAI and Anthropic. Google says the technology could improve natural lang
creative.content:
Google Research's Gemini-SQL2 turns natural language into executable SQL queries. Built on Gemini 3.1 Pro, it tops the BIRD benchmark at 80.04 percent accuracy, well ahead of OpenAI and Anthropic. Google says the technology could improve natural language features across its data services.
The article Google Research's Gemini-SQL2 tops text-to-SQL benchmarks by a wide margin appeared first on The Decoder.

creative.title: Microsoft's SkillOpt boosts GPT-5.5 by using nothing but a trained Markdown file
creative.summary: Microsoft and three Chinese universities have developed SkillOpt, a method that optimizes instruction documents for AI agents using principles from traditional model training. A simple Markdown file is enough to boost GPT-5.5 by about 23 points on pr
creative.content:
Microsoft and three Chinese universities have developed SkillOpt, a method that optimizes instruction documents for AI agents using principles from traditional model training. A simple Markdown file is enough to boost GPT-5.5 by about 23 points on procedural tasks, and the same file transfers across models and agent environments like Codex and Claude Code.
The article Microsoft's SkillOpt boosts GPT-5.5 by using nothing but a trained Markdown file appeared first on The Decoder.

creative.title: Claude Fable 5 outpaces GPT-5.5 by 13 points on FrontierMath's toughest problems
creative.summary: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 hits 88 percent accuracy on the hardest FrontierMath tier, a massive jump from Opus 4.5, which sat below 10 percent in early 2026. OpenAI's GPT-5.5 reaches about 75 percent on the same tier. The pace of improvement in AI ma
creative.content:
Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 hits 88 percent accuracy on the hardest FrontierMath tier, a massive jump from Opus 4.5, which sat below 10 percent in early 2026. OpenAI's GPT-5.5 reaches about 75 percent on the same tier. The pace of improvement in AI math keeps accelerating.
The article Claude Fable 5 outpaces GPT-5.5 by 13 points on FrontierMath's toughest problems appeared first on The Decoder.

culture.title: Anthropic to disable its most advanced AI models after US order limiting foreign access
culture.summary:
Company said US government believes safeguards can be bypassed and product used to identify software vulnerabilities
Anthropic said it will “abruptly disable” its most advanced AI models for all users after the US government ordered it to suspend acc
culture.content:
Company said US government believes safeguards can be bypassed and product used to identify software vulnerabilities
Anthropic said it will “abruptly disable” its most advanced AI models for all users after the US government ordered it to suspend access to the models for foreign nationals, citing national security concerns.
The company received the export control directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all foreign nationals, without being given specific details of the national se

culture.title: UK sets out AI infrastructure push at London Tech Week – how does it stack up?
culture.summary:
Government announces plans to invest billions, but questions linger over how its proposals on chips, social media and more will work
Ownership of the commanding heights of the AI economy is a political talking point around the world, as countries see
culture.content:
Government announces plans to invest billions, but questions linger over how its proposals on chips, social media and more will work
Ownership of the commanding heights of the AI economy is a political talking point around the world, as countries seek to assert some control of a technology dominated by the US and China.

culture.title: Pioneering UK Nerve Lab harnesses AI to map effect of children’s screen time
culture.summary:
Other projects include developing tools to help visually impaired people navigate video games 
 
Parents are constantly being told to limit their children’s screen time. But when it comes to deciphering which films or TV shows are best suited to deve
culture.content:
Other projects include developing tools to help visually impaired people navigate video games 
 
Parents are constantly being told to limit their children’s screen time. But when it comes to deciphering which films or TV shows are best suited to developing minds, the guidance remains largely one-size-fits-all. A relatively slow-paced programme such as Bluey offers a very different viewing experience to a fast-moving action series such as PAW Patrol, yet both are broadly considered suitable for y

culture.title: ‘Why would you put a toxic product into the hands of a young child?’: director turned activist Beeban Kidron on why big tech needs its ‘tobacco moment’
culture.summary:
In her work as an online safety campaigner, the baroness and Bridget Jones director has seen things she can never unsee – and she’s furious at the tech overlords doing nothing to stop the abuse
Through the open windows behind Beeban Kidron drifts the
culture.content:
In her work as an online safety campaigner, the baroness and Bridget Jones director has seen things she can never unsee – and she’s furious at the tech overlords doing nothing to stop the abuse
Through the open windows behind Beeban Kidron drifts the unmistakable sound of children playing.
